source.name: SSA Canonical Catalog
notes: Classic chickpea-tahini dip with lemon + garlic.
Designed as a baseline for adapters (salt/acid balance, texture targets, tool substitution).

yield.notes: Makes about 2 cups (approx. 450–520 g depending on thickness).

ingredients.label: Cooked chickpeas (drained; canned is fine)
ingredients.notes: If using 15-oz can: drain and rinse. Save 2–4 tbsp aquafaba (liquid) for thinning if desired.

ingredients.label: Tahini (sesame paste)
ingredients.notes: Stir well before measuring; separated tahini needs recombining.

ingredients.label: Fresh lemon juice
ingredients.notes: About 1 large lemon. Add more to taste.

ingredients.label: Garlic, minced
ingredients.notes: About 2 cloves. For milder flavor: briefly soak minced garlic in lemon juice 5 minutes.

ingredients.label: Ground cumin
ingredients.notes: Optional but traditional in many versions.

ingredients.label: Fine salt
ingredients.notes: Start here and adjust based on tahini + chickpea saltiness.

ingredients.label: Ice-cold water
ingredients.notes: Add gradually for smooth/whipped texture (or use chickpea aquafaba).

steps.title: Prep and (optional) peel chickpeas
steps.body: Drain and rinse chickpeas. For ultra-smooth hummus, rub chickpeas in a clean towel to loosen skins and discard as many skins as practical (optional). Set aside a splash of chickpea liquid (aquafaba) if you want to thin without water.

steps.title: Blend tahini, lemon, and garlic until creamy
steps.body: In a food processor (or blender), combine tahini, lemon juice, and minced garlic. Blend 30–60 seconds, scraping down as needed, until the mixture lightens and looks thick and creamy.

steps.title: Add chickpeas, salt, cumin, and olive oil
steps.body: Add chickpeas, salt, cumin (if using), and olive oil. Blend 60–90 seconds until it begins to look smooth. Scrape down the bowl halfway through.

steps.title: Adjust texture with ice water (or aquafaba)
steps.body: With the processor running, drizzle in ice-cold water (or aquafaba) 1 tbsp at a time until the hummus becomes smooth, creamy, and lightly whipped. Taste and adjust: more salt for depth, more lemon for brightness, or a little more tahini for richness.

steps.title: Serve and garnish
steps.body: Spoon into a bowl and swoosh the top with the back of a spoon. Drizzle with olive oil. Garnish with paprika and parsley if desired. Serve with pita, flatbread, cucumbers, or as part of a mezze plate.

steps.title: Store
steps.body: Refrigerate in a covered container up to 4–5 days. If it thickens, loosen with a teaspoon of water or lemon juice and stir. For best texture, bring to cool room temperature before serving.

refrigerate.notes: Surface can be protected with a thin drizzle of olive oil to reduce drying.

freeze.notes: Freeze up to 2 months. Thaw overnight in fridge; stir well and refresh with lemon/olive oil.

variations.title: Roasted garlic hummus
changes: Replace raw garlic with 1–2 roasted garlic cloves (or to taste).
Reduce lemon slightly, then adjust to taste.

variations.title: Roasted red pepper hummus
changes: Add 120 g roasted red peppers (drained) after blending chickpeas.
Add 1–2 g smoked paprika for depth.

variations.title: Ultra-smooth restaurant style
changes: Peel chickpeas or simmer chickpeas 10 minutes with a pinch of baking soda, then rinse well.
Blend longer and add ice water slowly to whip.

notes: Texture target: smooth enough to hold a swoosh but not paste-thick.
Acid and salt are adjustable; lemon brightness varies by fruit.
Tool fallback: if no food processor, use a blender and stop often to scrape; add extra liquid as needed.
